title: white-hacker security-policy gap report
description: A STRUCTURED gap report for an existing SECURITY.md / RFC 9116 security.txt, emitted by sec-policy (ADR-018, spike-08). The source files are UNTRUSTED DATA: this report contains ONLY booleans, a closed reporting-channel enum, section-name strings, and integer scores — NEVER any file-body span. additionalProperties:false (top level AND every nested object) GUARANTEES no raw policy text can ever leak through.

present.description: True when a SECURITY.md exists at one of the known locations.

path.description: Repo-relative path of the detected SECURITY.md, or null when absent.

sections.description: Presence of each best-practice section (heading match only).

missing_sections.description: Section-name strings absent from the policy (a subset of the seven keys).

reporting_channel.description: How vulnerabilities are reported, per the T-11.2 detector.

scorecard.description: OpenSSF Scorecard Security-Policy signals modeled structurally.

free_form_text.description: Substantive prose beyond a bare link/email (word-count threshold, URLs/emails excluded).

specific.description: >=2 vuln*/disclos* hits AND a numeric time expectation.

present.description: True when an RFC 9116 security.txt exists.

expired.description: True if the Expires field is in the past, False if valid, null when absent/unparseable.

injection_suspected.description: Denylist screen: True when any indirect-prompt-injection marker matched. A BOOLEAN signal only — no attacker text is echoed.
